Privacy Policy

Last updated: July 6, 2026 · Anchor

Draft — not yet reviewed by a lawyer. This document describes, honestly and specifically, what Anchor's code actually does with your data as of the date above. It has not had a legal review. Before this app takes real payments or is opened up to real users, have an actual attorney review this page, particularly around the crisis-detection disclosure below and any jurisdiction-specific requirements (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) that apply to where your users are.

What we collect

DataSourceWhy
Email address, password (or Google account info)Firebase AuthenticationSign-in. We never see your raw password — Firebase handles it.
XP, level, streak, completed lessonsOur servers (Firestore)To show your progress and sync it across devices.
Subscription status, Stripe customer IDStripeTo know whether you have Premium access.
Messages you send in conversationSent to Google's Gemini APITo generate a response. See "Your conversations," below.
IP addressOur server, temporarilyBasic abuse/rate-limit protection. Not written to a database.

Your conversations

When you talk with Anchor, your message is sent to Google's Gemini API to generate a response, and to a Bible-verse lookup that runs entirely on our own server (never a third party). We do not store your conversation transcripts in our own database. Messages exist for the length of your conversation and are sent to Google for processing under Google's Gemini API terms — we encourage you to read them if you want to understand Google's own retention practices.

A note on the crisis-detection feature

Anchor tries to notice when a conversation suggests real emotional distress and, when it does, responds with real crisis resources (in the US: call or text 988, or text HOME to 741741) instead of continuing the conversation as normal. This is an automated, imperfect feature, not a monitored safety service. Research on similar systems shows even well-built crisis detection misses real signal a meaningful fraction of the time. Anchor is not a substitute for a licensed mental health professional, and you should not rely on it as your only safety net. If you are in crisis, please contact a real person or service directly.

Payments

Stripe processes your payment. We never see or store your card number — Stripe's hosted checkout page handles that entirely. We only store your Stripe customer ID and whether your subscription is active, so we know whether to unlock Premium content for your account.

Who can see your data

Your progress data lives in a Firestore database only our server can read or write — nothing is publicly readable. We don't sell your data, and we don't share it with anyone except the service providers named on this page (Google Firebase, Google Gemini API, Stripe), each acting as a processor to make Anchor work.

Your choices

  • You can log out at any time.
  • To delete your account and associated progress data, contact us (see below) — we haven't yet built self-service account deletion.
  • You can cancel your Premium subscription at any time through Stripe's customer portal or by contacting us.

Children

Anchor is not directed at children under 13, and we don't knowingly collect data from anyone under 13. If you believe a child has created an account, contact us and we'll address it.

Changes to this policy

If this policy changes in a way that meaningfully affects how your data is used, we'll update the date at the top of this page.

Contact

Questions about this policy? [Add a real contact email before launch.]
